summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's lifecycle script (referenced from package.json line 35) and scanner.js perform host reconnaissance and post the results to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js requires os, https, fs, and child_process at the top, then uses os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), process.env, and filesystem enumeration (fs.existsSync over multiple paths) to collect installer identity, environment variables, and local file presence; the data is sent via https.request POST and curl invocations to https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-161, 298-323, 408). The same pattern is also wired into package.json (line 35) so that simply running `npm install` triggers the system-info exfiltration to the attacker-controlled GitHub Pages site. The package's purported purpose (wallet security checker) is a cover story; the only actual behavior on install/import is host fingerprinting and beaconing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
